author: Ameen Hassan
summary: Session opened with SessionStart hook successfully loading prior AgentOS context including open threads about hook registration status and RFP scoring architecture. The session contained no substantive new user interaction — it was a minimal session that immediately triggered a SessionEnd memory extraction pass. Prior context noted two tentative decisions around two-tier RFP scoring and 12 open questions, most notably whether install_hooks.sh had been run. No new work was completed or decisions confirmed this session.
problems: No substantive user interaction occurred this session to make progress on open threads
results: SessionStart hook loaded prior AgentOS context successfully
No new findings or conclusions — session was effectively empty of user-driven work
open_questions: Has install_hooks.sh been run to register SessionStart/SessionEnd hooks?
Are hooks now active and loading session memory correctly on SessionStart?
What weighted scoring factors apply after hard gates pass in RFP eligibility scoring?
What threshold scores indicate high vs. low priority opportunities?
How should the two-tier scoring logic be implemented in rfp_intelligence_pro?
